visible_bad_hover: to privilege someone with a bounty; to favour, privilege; to have; favouring, advantage; an advantage; bounty
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: Form II perfect active 1cp plus هُمْ object suffix must be visible before an authored contribution such as 'and We favored them' can be certified
what_failed: A populated hover used the entry/root family prose instead of the finite verb plus object-suffix contribution.
sarf_lesson: For finite derived verbs, record form, aspect, voice, subject agreement, proclitics, and object suffixes before accepting any hover.
nahw_lesson: The clause may refine favor/preference wording, but it cannot erase the هُمْ object contribution.
learner_explanation: وَفَضَّلْنَٰهُمْ contains وَ plus a Form II verb and final هُمْ. A learner needs to see 'We' and 'them', not only the root idea of favor.
drill_prompt: Segment وَفَضَّلْنَٰهُمْ into proclitic, Form II verb host, subject agreement, and object suffix; explain why the dictionary hover is unsafe.

visible_bad_hover: to privilege someone with a bounty; to favour, privilege; to have; favouring, advantage; an advantage; bounty
corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: passive Form II perfect 3mp must be preserved before a token gloss such as 'they were favored/preferred' can be certified
what_failed: The entry-family hover supplied active/root prose to a passive finite token.
sarf_lesson: Passive voice is visible morphology. Do not accept an active infinitive or broad entry gloss for a passive token.
nahw_lesson: Context may identify who is favored, but the passive construction controls the token contribution before broader phrase wording.
learner_explanation: فُضِّلُوا۟ is passive; it does not mean 'to favor'. The hover must show that the subject receives the action.
drill_prompt: Read فُضِّلُوا۟ for form, voice, and number, then write the unsafe hover that must be rejected.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: surface is finite verb يُحْيِي in this address, not the proper noun يَحْيَى; route to verb/POS review before hover certification
what_failed: A proper-name entry candidate collided with a finite verb surface because the row was not guarded by POS, harakah, and exact address.
sarf_lesson: Proper names and verbs with similar consonants must be separated by norm_strict, vocalization, POS, and context. A name entry cannot certify a finite verb token.
nahw_lesson: Referent context protects the sentence from a proper-name reading where the clause requires a verb.
learner_explanation: يُحْيِي here is a verb form, not the name يَحْيَى. The hover must not teach the Prophet's name for a verb token.
drill_prompt: Compare يَحْيَى and يُحْيِي. Which one is a proper noun and which one is a finite verb in 36:78? State the required gate.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: صَٰلِحًۭا must be classified as Prophet Ṣāliḥ or common/adjectival 'righteous/good' by exact context before hover authoring
what_failed: The source family mixes a proper-name entry with common active-participle/adjectival uses.
sarf_lesson: A proper noun and a common adjective can share the same surface family; neither direction is auto-safe without referent certification.
nahw_lesson: The surrounding āyah decides whether the token refers to the Prophet or describes a deed/person as righteous.
learner_explanation: صَالِح can be a name or an adjective. The hover must match this exact āyah, not just the entry family.
drill_prompt: For one صَالِح row, identify whether the verse speaks about the Prophet or a righteous action, then choose name/common or pending.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: attached bāʾ plus majrūr nominal دَيْنٍ needs a PP/nominal hover such as 'for/with a debt' only after attachment review
what_failed: A preposition plus governed noun inherited a verb-derived gloss and lost the bāʾ relation and nominal case role.
sarf_lesson: A noun after an attached preposition is not certified by a verb/root entry. Preserve the host noun shape and visible preposition.
nahw_lesson: Jar-majrūr attachment decides the exact English relation, so the row needs nahw review before propagation.
learner_explanation: بِدَيْنٍ includes بِـ and a noun. A learner should see the preposition relation, not a verb infinitive.
drill_prompt: Segment بِدَيْنٍ and explain why 'to be indebted to' is not a rich token hover.
